markets.no_sub_title: Max Verstappen
markets.rules_primary: If Max Verstappen set the fastest valid qualifying lap time in the qualifying session that determines the starting grid for the Australian Grand Prix Grand Prix originally scheduled for March 7, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: For the purposes of this market, Pole Position is awarded to the driver who records the fastest official lap time in the Qualifying session (Q3) of the Grand Prix, as determined by the FIA’s published classification. If Qualifying does not progress to Q3 (e.g., weather, red flags, or format changes), Pole Position will be awarded based on the FIA’s official qualifying results for that event. Pole Position for this market is determined exclusively by the official qualifying classification and is not impacted by the final starting grid; any subsequent grid penalties or starting-order adjustments do not change who is deemed to have achieved Pole Position. Sprint Race qualifying sessions are not considered for this market.

markets.no_sub_title: Tie
markets.rules_primary: If Tie is the result of the second half of regulation time in the Atlanta vs Milwaukee professional basketball game originally scheduled for Mar 4, 2026, then the market resolves to Yes.
markets.rules_secondary: The following market refers to the team that scores the most points during the specified half. Only points scored during the specified half of play count towards this market. If both teams score the same amount of points during the half, all team strikes resolve to No and the Tie strike resolves to Yes. If this game is postponed or delayed, the market will remain open and close after the rescheduled game has finished (within two weeks).
